headline: Golfer season stats no longer count in-progress tournaments

points.text: root cause: `syncLiveStandings` writes *projected* earnings/positions into `tournamentResult` during play, and [`getSeasonStats`](#diff-4) aggregated those rows as if they were final
the fix is one `WHERE` clause — `tournament.status in (COMPLETED, FINALIZING)` — matching the filter the earnings rollup already used
the season-results table still lists the live tournament; [the page](#diff-3) now marks that row with a green **Live** badge and an `est.` earnings label
no new tests: existing golfer-service tests mock the repo, so coverage is typecheck + the PR test plan's live-tournament verification
bundled along for the ride: a [live-scorecard idea doc](#diff-1) with zero runtime impact

facets.what: Filter golfer season stats to settled tournaments; visually flag the live row instead
facets.why: Projected mid-round data inflated stats — e.g. best finish "1st" after round 1, with millions in unearned money
facets.size: 5 files, +102/−2 (~40 runtime lines; 62 lines are a docs idea)

risk.note: one additive WHERE clause with an existing rollup precedent; the rest is UI labeling; no schema changes

diagram.title: How projected data leaked into season stats — and where the filter now sits

title: Feature home — the golfer page read path and the live-sync write path
The root cause and the fix — one WHERE clause
description: The bug lived in a single query. `syncLiveStandings` keeps `tournamentResult` rows updated with *projected* numbers while a tournament is `IN_PROGRESS` — that's a feature for standings, but `getSeasonStats` aggregated those same rows with no status filter, so the golfer profile treated projections as history.

blocks.title: golfer.ts
blocks.description:
**The one-line root-cause fix — `getSeasonStats` now aggregates only settled tournaments.**

- adds `.where("tournament.status", "in", ["COMPLETED", "FINALIZING"])` to the stats query — the exact filter the earnings rollup already applies in `repositories/earnings.ts`, so stats and standings now agree on what counts
- effect on each stat: `totalEarnings` drops projected money, `bestFinish` no longer reports a live leader's "1st", `eventsPlayed` no longer counts an unfinished event
- `FINALIZING` stays included on purpose: play is over and projected earnings are held until official results land, so those numbers are near-final rather than mid-round noise
- the sibling `getSeasonResults` query is deliberately untouched — the results table should still show the live tournament, just [clearly labeled](#diff-3)
- coverage note: the existing service tests mock this repo, so the new filter rides on typecheck plus the PR test plan's next-live-tournament verification

diagram.title: Tournament lifecycle — which statuses feed season stats
diagram.d2:
direction: right
"getSeasonStats": { class: changed }
UPCOMING -> IN_PROGRESS: first tee shot
IN_PROGRESS -> FINALIZING: play ends
FINALIZING -> COMPLETED: official results
IN_PROGRESS -> "getSeasonStats": was counted — now excluded
FINALIZING -> "getSeasonStats": counted
COMPLETED -> "getSeasonStats": counted
diagram.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  UPCOMING -->|first tee shot| IN_PROGRESS
  IN_PROGRESS -->|play ends| FINALIZING
  FINALIZING -->|official results| COMPLETED
  IN_PROGRESS -->|was counted — now excluded| STATS[getSeasonStats]
  FINALIZING -->|counted| STATS
  COMPLETED -->|counted| STATS
  classDef changed fill:#ffd43b,stroke:#f08c00,color:#1b1b1b,stroke-width:2px;
  class STATS changed;

hunks.header: @@ -49,6 +49,7 @@ export async function getSeasonStats(golferId: string, season: number) {

title: The UI half — make the live row honest
description: Filtering the stats leaves the live tournament visible only in the season-results table — where its projected numbers could still be mistaken for finals. These two diffs mark that row so a reader can't confuse projection with history.

blocks.title: page.tsx
blocks.description:
**The season-results table now tells the truth about a live row.**

- new `isLive` flag on `tournamentStatus === "IN_PROGRESS"` drives all three tweaks
- the row gets `styles.rowLive` (faint green tint) plus a green **Live** badge beside the tournament name
- projected money is labeled with a small `est.` under the earnings figure
- `isFirst` now requires `!isLive` — a golfer leading mid-round no longer gets the gold 1st-place styling, the visible face of the same bug
- `missedCut` also gains `!isLive` as an explicit guard (its status check already excluded live rows; this makes the intent readable)

blocks.title: golfer.module.css
blocks.description:
**Three new classes in the existing "Row states" section back the live treatment.**

- `.rowLive` — 4% green `color-mix` row tint, with the earnings cell muted to read as provisional
- `.liveBadge` — uppercase green pill using the design tokens (`--color-green`, `--text-xs`, `--radius-sm`)
- `.estLabel` — small muted `est.` line rendered under the earnings amount

title: Along for the ride — a live-scorecard idea doc
description: Written while working in the live-tournament area of the golfer page; pure documentation with zero runtime impact on this fix.

blocks.title: live-scorecard.md
blocks.description:
**Idea doc: hole-by-hole scorecard on the golfer page during live tournaments.**

- documents ESPN's undocumented per-player scorecard endpoint (one call per golfer, per-round hole data)
- weighs three approaches: fetch-on-demand (A), persist per-round totals (B), full per-hole storage (C, ~10K rows per tournament)
- recommends Option A — no schema changes, fetch only during `IN_PROGRESS` tournaments
- open questions on rate limits, a 5-minute cache TTL, and whether to scope to the user's picked golfer

lines: +During a live tournament, the golfer page only shows overall score-to-par and projected position/earnings. Users can't see how their golfer is performing hole-by-hole without leaving the app.
+Show a golf scorecard (hole-by-hole scores per round) on the golfer detail page when a tournament is in progress. The classic 18-hole grid with par, player score, and birdie/bogey indicators.
+- Returns hole-by-hole data for each round (par, score, over/under per hole)
+- **Per-player** — one API call per golfer, not per field
+- Same undocumented status as the other ESPN endpoints we use
+### Option A: Fetch on demand, don't persist
+Fetch the scorecard API when viewing a golfer page during a live tournament. Display inline, no new DB tables.
+- Pros: Simple, no schema changes, always fresh
+- Cons: Extra API call per page load, no historical scorecards after tournament ends
+### Option B: Store per-round scores (not per-hole)
+We already receive `linescores` (per-round totals like 68, 71, 70) from the scoreboard API during `syncLiveStandings`. Store these in a new column or table.
+- Pros: Minimal schema change, round scores persist after tournament
+### Option C: Store full scorecard data
+New `scorecard` table with hole-by-hole scores. Sync during live standings updates.
+- Pros: Full historical data, rich display options
+- Cons: 18 holes × 4 rounds × ~150 players = ~10K rows per tournament, more complex sync

+Start with **Option A** — fetch on demand during live tournaments only. It requires no schema changes and gives users the most useful view (how their golfer is doing right now). If there's demand for historical scorecards, move to Option C later.
+- Show below the season results table (or expand the live row) during IN_PROGRESS tournaments
+- Standard scorecard layout: Out (1-9), In (10-18), Total
+- Show current round prominently, previous rounds collapsible
+- Does ESPN rate-limit the scorecard endpoint? Need to test.
+- Should we cache scorecard responses (e.g., 5-minute TTL) to reduce API calls?
+- Do we show scorecards for all golfers or only the user's picked golfer?
- [Magic Link Notifications](./ideas/magic-link-notifications.md) — Pre-authenticated links in notification emails
- [Data Freshness Indicator](./ideas/data-freshness-indicator.md) — Show "Last updated" timestamp so users know data is current
- [Standings Current Tournament](./ideas/standings-current-tournament.md) — Show which tournament standings are current through
+- [Live Scorecard](./ideas/live-scorecard.md) — Hole-by-hole scorecard on golfer page during live tournaments
